task_completion.description: Open the human insulin gene in UGENE
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface is open and displaying the 'human_insulin_gene.gb' sequence.

task_completion.description: Edit the sequence to introduce the P28D mutation
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE sequence editor showing the mutated motif 'TACACAGACAAG' (or specifically the 'GAC' codon replacing the original 'CCC' codon) in the DNA sequence.

task_completion.description: Add an annotation for the mutated bases
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE annotations tree or feature editor showing a new 'misc_feature' or 'variation' feature at the mutated site, with a 'note' qualifier containing the text 'P28D'.

task_completion.description: Save the engineered sequence to the results directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog in UGENE, a file manager, or terminal output showing 'insulin_aspart_mutant.gb' successfully saved in the '~/UGENE_Data/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a text report documenting the mutation details
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'mutagenesis_report.txt' located in '~/UGENE_Data/results/'. The text must explicitly mention the original codon (CCC), the engineered codon (GAC), and the biological purpose of the mutation (e.g., fast-acting insulin analog, preventing hexamer formation).

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE graphical interface for sequence manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with UGENE's sequence editor and annotation tools, rather than modifying the .gb file directly in a text editor or via a terminal script.

integrity.description: The sequence length remains unchanged to prevent frameshifts
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence length indicator in UGENE shows exactly 469 bp after the mutation is performed, confirming no bases were accidentally inserted or deleted.

integrity.description: The agent actively authored the mutagenesis report
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing or generating the report in a text editor or via terminal commands, providing actual biological context rather than just echoing the task prompt verbatim.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The wild-type sequence contains the motif 'TACACACCCAAG' (encoding the YTPK peptide). The correct P28D mutation replaces the Proline codon (CCC) with an Aspartic Acid codon (GAC), resulting in the mutant motif 'TACACAGACAAG'. The original sequence length is exactly 469 bp, which must be maintained to avoid frameshifts. The expected output files are ~/UGENE_Data/results/insulin_aspart_mutant.gb and ~/UGENE_Data/results/mutagenesis_report.txt.
